title: elymus lanceolatus subsp. yukonensis
intro: Elymus lanceolatus subsp. yukonensis, also known as Yukon wildrye, is a subspecies of the Elymus lanceolatus plant, a type of wild grass native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to help reduce inflammation and prevent infections. The health benefits of Elymus lanceolatus subsp. yukonensis include reducing fever and soothing digestive issues, and it has been used to treat various health conditions such as colds, flu, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Elymus lanceolatus subsp. yukonensis is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
